_meta.source_paper: Appiah, A. (2026). Negotiating Intelligence: African Agency in the Global AI Value Chain. Working paper, prepared for submission to Data & Policy (Cambridge University Press).
_meta.scope: v2.1 — country-level composite scores from the master tracker (19 countries × 44 indicators = 836 scored data points).
_meta.notes: Labels (dx, dy, anchor) are hand-tuned offsets to prevent collisions on the plane. Map coordinates are fallback values for the simplified circle map; production should use TopoJSON feature IDs instead.

countries.name: Senegal
countries.narrative: Strong international engagement profile — GPAI member, early Malabo ratification, Paris Statement signatory, Smart Africa leadership — without corresponding domestic enforcement or procurement capacity. The widest formal–substantive gap in the sample.

countries.name: Egypt
countries.narrative: The most elaborate formal architecture in the sample: a presidentially endorsed 74-page strategy, an operational data protection law, UN Scientific Panel representation, and the first Arab and African OECD AI Principles adherent. Substantive capacity has not followed at the same pace.

countries.name: Morocco
countries.narrative: The CNDP has existed for sixteen years without issuing a single fine, despite holding statutory authority to impose criminal penalties. A textbook case of despotic power without infrastructural power.

countries.name: Rwanda
countries.narrative: Closest approximation to procurement sovereignty in the sample. Engagements with Zipline and Anthropic include local knowledge transfer and capacity-building as conditions of engagement. First country globally with nationwide autonomous drone delivery.

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.narrative: The FCCPC tribunal's $220 million ruling against Meta in 2025 is the most consequential enforcement action in African data governance history. Substantive score of 53 is the highest in West Africa, driven by enforcement trajectory and a deep deployment ecosystem — Zipline, mPharma, Helium Health, CAD4TB.

countries.name: Kenya
countries.narrative: One of two African states in the negotiated interdependence zone, alongside South Africa. The ODPC suspended Worldcoin's biometric collection in 2023, has issued more than 110 decisions, and is extending its geographic reach through county offices. The only African country with a publicly quantified AI strategy budget: KES 152 billion.

countries.name: Ghana
countries.narrative: Active deployment ecosystem including Zipline medical drone delivery across multiple regions. Formal architecture moderate but enforcement capacity has not developed at pace.

countries.name: Botswana
countries.narrative: Limited formal architecture and limited substantive capacity. The gap reflects underinvestment on both axes rather than active divergence between declaration and execution.

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.narrative: The Ethiopian AI Institute manages 26 projects internally, including Mela — a locally trained large language model for Ethiopian languages. State-directed indigenous capacity pathway, contrasting with Tunisia's ecosystem model.

countries.name: Cameroon
countries.narrative: Among the lowest scores on both dimensions. Illustrative of the dependency by default baseline — neither formal architecture nor substantive capacity is under active construction.

countries.name: Gabon
countries.narrative: The CNPDCP was physically denied access to a company's administrative documents during a regulatory inspection. Sovereignty theatre at its most literal: a formal structure that dissolves on contact with the entities it is meant to regulate.

countries.name: Malawi
countries.narrative: The data protection authority, housed within the Communications Regulatory Authority, was investigated by the Malawi Human Rights Commission for procuring mass surveillance ahead of elections. A structural conflict no legislative amendment can resolve.

countries.name: Sierra Leone
countries.narrative: Low scores on both dimensions reflect a state where the institutional preconditions for meaningful AI governance — regulatory capacity, technical expertise, fiscal space — are themselves under construction.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.narrative: One of two African states in the negotiated interdependence zone, alongside Kenya. The Information Regulator has pursued criminal prosecutions yielding prison sentences. The Competition Commission treats data accumulation as a metric of market dominance — bridging data governance and competition policy.

countries.name: Tunisia
countries.narrative: Formal architecture now above the coherence threshold following strategy publication and institutional development, but substantive capacity lags. Produced InstaDeep — acquired by BioNTech for £362 million up front and up to £200 million in earn-out — and hosts more than 120 AI startups, multiple NVIDIA DGX deployments, and TunBERT. The ecosystem outpaces the state architecture that would govern it.

countries.name: France
countries.narrative: External calibration point. Formal and substantive capacity closely matched at high absolute levels — the configuration the framework treats as the target zone.

countries.name: Japan
countries.narrative: External calibration point. Substantive capacity exceeds formal architecture — a mature deployment and regulatory ecosystem that has not leaned heavily on strategy-document signalling.

countries.name: UAE
countries.narrative: External calibration point. Both axes at high absolute levels, closely aligned around the coherence diagonal.

countries.name: Brazil
countries.narrative: External calibration point. Substantive capacity meaningfully exceeds formal architecture — the largest below-diagonal gap in the reference set.

sub_dimensions.name: National AI Strategy
sub_dimensions.description: Presence, scope, and governmental endorsement of a formal national AI strategy.

sub_dimensions.name: Data Protection Legal Framework
sub_dimensions.description: Comprehensive data protection legislation covering personal data, automated decision-making, and cross-border transfers.

sub_dimensions.name: Institutional Architecture
sub_dimensions.description: Formal bodies with jurisdiction over AI governance: data protection authorities, AI councils, sector regulators.

sub_dimensions.name: International AI Engagement
sub_dimensions.description: Membership in international AI governance forums (GPAI, OECD Principles, Paris Statement, UN panels).

sub_dimensions.name: Continental AI Commitments
sub_dimensions.description: Ratification of African Union frameworks including Malabo Convention and the Continental AI Strategy.

sub_dimensions.name: Enforcement Capacity
sub_dimensions.description: Demonstrated ability of regulators to investigate, sanction, and litigate against major AI providers and data controllers.

sub_dimensions.name: Procurement Power
sub_dimensions.description: State capacity to negotiate AI procurement contracts with switching rights, audit access, and knowledge transfer clauses.

sub_dimensions.name: Assurance Infrastructure
sub_dimensions.description: Institutional capacity to evaluate, audit, and certify AI systems deployed in-country.

sub_dimensions.name: Delivery Capability
sub_dimensions.description: Demonstrated ability of the state to deploy AI-enabled public services at scale.

sub_dimensions.name: Negotiation Capability
sub_dimensions.description: State capacity to renegotiate existing AI deployments, partnerships, and licensing arrangements on favourable terms.

sub_dimensions.name: Epistemic Alignment
sub_dimensions.description: Reserved for future scoring. Measures whether deployed AI systems reflect or displace local knowledge systems.

indicators._note: Placeholder. The full 44-indicator set with rubrics will be loaded at v2 from Akwasi's scoring workbook. Each indicator will have a slug, name, parent sub_dimension, rubric (0-3), and display order.

scores._note: Empty at v1. The 660-row scoring grid (44 indicators × 15 African countries) is loaded from the scoring workbook at v2.

sources._note: Empty at v1. Citations accompany scores and are loaded together at v2.
